data.body:
ADR-0042 DRAFT (captured now while research + context are fresh; S6 lands this as docs/adr/0042-*.md).

# ADR 0042 — Parallel-agent auto-lander for `main` (serial Phase 1)

## Status
Accepted (design); implementation staged under epic `peridotite-thickset-pintail` (f1fa).

## Context
`main` is Fast-Forward-Only (ADR-0040). Parallel agent sessions race to land; the loser must
manually rebase-to-tip and retry, and with a feature **stack** this burns wall-clock (CI re-runs
on non-conflicting changes) and agent tokens/attention. **This is a live, active pain.** We want
agents to set an opt-in intent and walk away; changes land autonomously or are handed back with a
single actionable outcome — without ever weakening the two-vote gate.

## Decision
A single-instance **serial auto-lander** (project infra, Gerrit + GitHub-Actions specific): select
the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain → rebase to tip → await a fresh `Verified` on every
rebased member → ancestor-atomic submit. On failure the whole stack is handed back to its owner.

## The R5 safety invariant (never weakened)
The tested tree IS the merged tree: FFO backstop + a fresh `Verified` required on each rebased
member's own SHA + the bot casts NO votes + a pre-submit re-confirmation. This is the same
guarantee Zuul/Chromium-CQ gating provides.

## Key design decisions (prior art: Zuul, LUCI CV / Chromium CQ, Gerrit primitives, Prow/Tide)
1. **Intent = an `Autosubmit` label**, requester-votable by `Contributors` (agents set it under
   their own identity; the bot never votes). Analogue: CQ's `Commit-Queue+2`.
2. **Status surface (D1):** per-change outcome reported BACK onto the Gerrit change (comment +
   label state), read via Gerrit creds `land` already holds (CQ pattern); bot liveness + queue via
   a read-only HTTP status endpoint exposing `heartbeat_age_s` (Zuul `zuul-web` / Tide `deck`).
3. **Failure hand-back (D2):** remove `Autosubmit` + post a comment naming the manual step + write
   the S3 marker (CQ "reset" / Zuul dequeue-and-comment). Label-removal is the "handed back" signal.
4. **Metrics (D3):** emit a RAW `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` timer (individual `Value`
   datapoints); percentiles via CloudWatch extended statistics downstream — no hard-coded buckets
   (Zuul `resident_time`).
5. **Hang recovery (D4):** plain-compose has no K8s liveness probe, so a HEALTHCHECK marks the
   container unhealthy on a stale local heartbeat and the `willfarrell/autoheal` sidecar restarts it.
6. **Gerrit client (D5):** reuse this repo's STDLIB-ONLY client pattern (a thin helper in
   `infra/autolander`), NOT `pygerrit2` (would add a dependency the repo deliberately avoids) and
   NOT a `src/rebar` import (keeps core platform-agnostic).
7. **Credentials (D6):** service-user HTTP password (SSM) + `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (keeps
   the human as uploader, forces CI re-verify); `land` uses the agent's ambient Gerrit credential.
8. **Emergency-stop + drain (D7):** a state-volume sentinel file → `paused` (stop consuming) +
   graceful drain (Zuul `pause`/`graceful`), with a crash-safe recovery record on restart.

## Separation of concerns
Landing is platform-specific + maintainer-owned: it lives in `infra/` + a project CLI, may
`import rebar` only for ticket ops, and adds NO Gerrit landing logic to `src/rebar` core.

## Reliability posture + accepted tradeoffs
Single-writer (flock) + heartbeat + autoheal + loud error metric + stuck-state (time-in-phase)
alarm + a crash-safe recovery record reconciled against live Gerrit on restart (no double-submit,
no stranded stack). The single instance is an accepted SPOF for Phase 1 (its outage degrades to the
manual status quo, surfaced immediately as `lander_down`).

## Deferred — and the triggers that promote them
- **Provider-seam (idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`):** a platform-agnostic Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab landing
  adapter in rebar core. Promote when a SECOND platform needs to land; it will unify the review-bot
  client and the autolander helper behind one seam (Zuul-driver / go-gerrit-vendoring model).
- **Bounded-speculation batching (idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`):** Phase-2 throughput. Cross-stack
  batching was spiked and REJECTED on this infra (per-patchset CI → N runs not one; landing N off
  one run needs a GerriScary-class direct Verified cast). Promote bounded speculation (≤ the GHA
  concurrency cap, each candidate verified on its own clean tree) ONLY if the serial treadmill
  becomes a MEASURED pain.
- **HA (external state store):** replace the single instance + in-memory `wipChain` with an
  externalized queue/lock (Zuul's scale-out lesson). Promote if single-instance availability
  becomes insufficient.
